title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville in Boston draws lines due to its unique 'one size fits all' clothing policy.
Blank Street coffee on Boylston Street employs security to manage frequent long lines.
Brick Street Bagels and North End pastry shops regularly feature queues due to high demand.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, 40, pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at her two Massachusetts spas.
Fadanelli admitted to importing fake Botox and fillers from China and Brazil, using them without a medical license.
She faces up to 20 years in prison and numerous fines for her actions, collecting over $1 million from clients.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Police fatally shot Jacob Graves, 29, during a mental health crisis after he attacked officers near Northeastern University.
Graves, a former Berklee College of Music student, called 911 believing he was threatened by armed individuals.
Multiple officers and emergency responders were injured; the incident is under investigation by Boston police and the DA's office.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens remains committed to his role with the Boston Celtics, dismissing college basketball job rumors.
Stevens has been mentioned in coaching rumors, including with UNC before they hired Mike Malone.
Stevens joined the Celtics in 2013 and serves as president of basketball operations since Danny Ainge's departure.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, was sentenced to life with parole possible after 15 years for the 2024 murder of Maceo Martin.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder after a nine-day trial for shooting Martin outside a Cambridge apartment.
The shooting occurred when Martin was shouting at his ex-wife, who was inside with their children and Lapaix.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense asked for a two-phase trial, proposing she admit to killing her children if granted.
Judge William Sullivan previously denied the motion, ruling guilt and criminal responsibility should be considered together.
Prosecutors oppose splitting the trial, insisting on presenting the case as a whole; trial set for July 20.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade linebacker Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round draft pick.
Patriots include a 2027 seventh-round pick in the trade deal with the Texans.
Trade creates $1.5 million cap space and implies further upgrades to Patriots' linebacker corps.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Community College of Rhode Island's Warwick campus was locked down after reports of a suspicious man with a possible weapon.
Police found a backpack containing an airsoft gun and pellet rifle in the search near Commonwealth Avenue.
Classes were canceled for the day as authorities continue to search for the suspect, whom they have identified.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from knee and rib injuries faces setbacks, delaying his return to the Red Sox lineup.
Manager Alex Cora reports Casas struggled to swing and will undergo imaging to assess his rib muscle strain.
Casas' injuries hinder his comeback, having played only 92 games since 2024 after a strong 2023 season.

title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Trump's new ballroom project at the White House includes a substantial military bunker beneath it.
The bunker will feature bomb shelters, medical facilities, secure communication systems, and defenses against bioweapons.
A judge halted the project citing a need for congressional approval, but necessary security-related construction continues.
